question: What is the official mascot of the Commonwealth Table Tennis Championships 2026?
detail:
- **Mayur** was unveiled in Delhi as the official mascot of the **Commonwealth Table Tennis Championships 2026**.
- It was unveiled by Delhi's Chief Minister **Rekha Gupta**.
- Mayur is inspired by India's National Bird, the **Peacock**, and stands for Energy, Hospitality and Unity.
- The Championship runs **27 July – 2 August 2026** at **Thyagaraj Stadium, New Delhi**. This is the **22nd edition**, hosted jointly by the Delhi Government and the **Table Tennis Federation of India (TTFI)**, with 35+ countries expected.
- **About the Championships:** a biennial (every 2 years) event among Commonwealth nations. First edition — **1971**; first host — **Singapore**; organised by the **Commonwealth Table Tennis Federation (CTTF)**.
- **About TTFI:** the national governing body for table tennis in India. Established **1926**, headquarters **New Delhi**, President **Meghna Ahlawat** (2026).

question: Vice-President C. P. Radhakrishnan released the book "The Voice of Justice — Justice Gavai Speaks". Justice B. R. Gavai was which Chief Justice of India (CJI)?
detail:
- Vice-President **C. P. Radhakrishnan** released the book **"The Voice of Justice — Justice Gavai Speaks"** at Uprashtrapati Bhavan, New Delhi.
- The book is a collection of the speeches, lectures and reflections of **Justice Bhushan Ramkrishna (B. R.) Gavai**, the **52nd Chief Justice of India**. It was edited by **Prof. (Dr.) S. Sivakumar**.
- He took charge on **14 May 2025** and retired on **23 November 2025**.
- **Important judgments he was part of:** the **Article 370 case (2023)** — the Constitution Bench that upheld the Centre's decision to remove Article 370 from Jammu & Kashmir; the **Electoral Bonds case (2024)** — the verdict declaring the Electoral Bond Scheme unconstitutional; and the **Demonetisation case (2023)** — the bench that held the 2016 demonetisation valid.

question: After 60 years, the Indian Grey Hornbill bird has been reintroduced to Gir National Park. In which state is this national park located?
detail:
- The **Indian Grey Hornbill** — called **"Chilotro"** in Gujarat — has been successfully reintroduced to the **Gir Forest, Gujarat** after more than 60 years.
- A scientific study found the bird is now surviving in the wild and **breeding** as well.
- Under the programme **40 Indian Grey Hornbills** were released in two phases, with **satellite transmitters** used to monitor their movement, habitat use and breeding behaviour.
- **About the bird:** scientific name **Ocyceros birostris**, of the **Bucerotidae** family, identified by its curved bill and the **casque** above it. It lives mainly in dry deciduous forests, open woodlands and urban parks, and eats fruits, insects, small reptiles and seeds. It spreads seeds over long distances and so plays a big role in forest regeneration — which is why it is called the **"Forest Gardener"**. **IUCN status: Least Concern (LC)**.
- **Gir National Park:** located in **Gujarat**; a National Park & Wildlife Sanctuary; famous as the only natural habitat of the **Asiatic Lion**.

question: When is the foundation day of the Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) celebrated?
detail:
- **ICAR Day** is celebrated every year on **16 July**.
- On this day in **1929**, the **Indian Council of Agricultural Research** was established as the **Imperial Council of Agricultural Research**.
- ICAR is India's **largest agricultural research organisation**. Its main work is to promote agricultural research, agricultural education and extension services.
- **About ICAR:** established **16 July 1929**; headquarters **New Delhi**; under the **Ministry of Agriculture**.

question: Who was honoured with the Fortune India Most Powerful Women (MPW) Honour 2026?
detail:
- **Nita Ambani**, Founder & Chairperson of the **Reliance Foundation**, was honoured with the **Fortune India Most Powerful Women (MPW) Honour** in July 2026.
- The honour was given for her contribution to **Leadership, Institution Building and Inclusive Development**, at the Fortune India MPW event held in **Mumbai, Maharashtra**.
- **About the MPW Honour:** a prestigious award given by Fortune India to recognise India's most powerful women leaders — chosen for notable work in business, leadership, innovation, entrepreneurship and social impact.
- **About Fortune India:** a leading Indian business magazine publishing reports and lists on business, economy, industry, leadership and corporate rankings. Founded **2010**.

question: India's first Trial Index of Services Production (ISP) has been released by which ministry?
options: Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI)
detail:
- The **Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI)** released India's first **Trial Index of Services Production (ISP)**.
- Just as the **IIP (Index of Industrial Production)** shows how much industry is producing, the **ISP** shows how much the **services sector** — banks, hotels, transport, telecom, trade and so on — is doing, and whether its activity is rising or falling.
- It is India's **first high-frequency (monthly) index** measuring the performance of the services sector. It is based on **April 2026** statistics, with **2024–25** as the base year.
- It covers **19 service sub-sectors**, which together make up about **60%** of India's services sector.
- **April 2026 highlights:** **14 of the 19** sub-sectors recorded double-digit growth; the highest growth was **37.2%** in **Accommodation & Food Services**.
- **About MoSPI:** the ministry that prepares, compiles and publishes India's official statistics. Minister — **Rao Inderjit Singh**.

question: Which Integrated Digital Healthcare Platform has been launched by the Government of Tamil Nadu?
detail:
- The **Tamil Nadu Government** launched an integrated digital healthcare platform called **Nalam TN** in Chennai.
- It was inaugurated by **Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay** at Presidency College, Chennai.
- Nalam TN brings the state's various government health services onto a **single digital platform** for patients, hospitals and government institutions, making access to healthcare easier and faster. It comes under the **Health & Family Welfare Department**.
- **Related initiative — NalamAI:** a **WhatsApp-based health chatbot** started by the Tamil Nadu government, through which people can use government hospital services on WhatsApp itself. Its main purpose is **OPD appointment booking** from home, plus giving patients easy access to hospital information and services.

question: Which state has announced the establishment of India's first Government-driven Artificial Intelligence (AI) University?
detail:
- The **Karnataka Government** announced the establishment of India's first **Government-driven Artificial Intelligence (AI) University**.
- The announcement was made on **14 July 2026** in **Bengaluru**, during the inauguration of **Google I/O Connect India 2026**.
- The university aims to promote education, research and innovation in **AI, Machine Learning, Robotics, Data Science** and other emerging technologies.
- An **AI Hub** will also be set up in the state to support startups, researchers and technology companies.
- **"Government-driven" means** the university's establishment, running and funding will all be done by the government. The **AI Hub** will be an innovation & incubation centre giving AI startups, researchers and technology companies facilities for research, development and collaboration.

question: Which Indian men's pair became World No. 2 in the ITTF Men's Doubles World Rankings?
detail:
- **Manav Thakkar** and **Manush Shah** created history by reaching **World No. 2** in the **ITTF Men's Doubles World Rankings**.
- This is the **best-ever world ranking** by any Indian men's table tennis pair. The pair was previously at World No. 3.
- Both are mentored by Indian table tennis player **Sharath Kamal**.
- Currently China's **Lin Shidong and Huang Youzheng** are World No. 1.
- **Previous best by an Indian pair:** Archana Kamath and Manika Batra — World No. 4 in Women's Doubles, 2022.
- **About ITTF:** the international governing body responsible for running table tennis worldwide, making rules, releasing world rankings and organising international competitions. Established **1926**; headquarters **Lausanne, Switzerland**; President **Petra Sörling** (Sweden).

question: Where was the NATO Summit 2026 held?
detail:
- The **NATO Summit 2026** was held in **Ankara, Türkiye**, chaired by NATO Secretary-General **Mark Rutte**.
- This was the **second NATO Summit hosted by Türkiye** — the earlier one was in **Istanbul in 2004**.
- The summit put special emphasis on **Defence Industry, Drone Technology, Joint Procurement** and technology-based defence innovation.
- The **previous NATO Summit** was held on **24–26 June 2025** in **The Hague, Netherlands**.
- **About the NATO Summit:** the highest-level meeting of the Heads of State & Government of NATO member countries, where they take key decisions on global security, defence cooperation and the alliance's future strategy.
- **About NATO:** a military alliance founded to strengthen collective defence and security cooperation among members. Established **4 April 1949** (by 12 countries); headquarters **Brussels, Belgium**; **32 members** (32nd — Sweden, 2024); Secretary-General **Mark Rutte**; founding treaty — the **North Atlantic Treaty (Washington Treaty)**.
- **Note:** the **2027** NATO Summit is proposed to be held in **Tirana**, the capital of Albania.
